business_problem: Amagi supports 8,000+ streaming channels, 300+ distributors, and 26B annual ad impressions for 45% of the world's top media companies, but its legacy stack (Dataproc for batch, Snowflake for analytics, in-house platforms) created data silos with separate governance models, conflicting numbers across Finance, Operations, and Product, and complex cross-region governance across AWS and GCP.
solution_summary: Amagi rebuilt its data platform as the Amagi Data Platform (ADP) on Databricks, consolidating Dataproc, Snowflake, and multiple in-house systems into a single Lakehouse spanning AWS and GCP. Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines ingest content metadata, asset catalogs, advertising data, service streams, program schedules, and user data into governed Delta Lake tables, where Unity Catalog enforces lineage, access controls, sensitive-data tagging, and cross-cloud compliance. Finance, Operations, and Product teams query the same source of truth through Databricks SQL warehouses, Genie natural-language sessions, and notebooks running on serverless compute, while customer-facing data products for streaming platforms, ad-tech partners, and content owners are derived from the same governed Lakehouse, eliminating reconciliation between systems.
business_outcome: Consolidating Dataproc, Snowflake, and in-house platforms onto Databricks across AWS and GCP cut costs by 45%, sped time-to-market for new data products by 15x, and reduced production incidents by 35% — with a single governed source of truth that aligns Finance, Operations, Product, and customer-facing reporting.
metrics: 15x faster time to market for new data products
architecture_clues: Unified lakehouse on Databricks with Unity Catalog governance
Internal access via notebooks, Genie, SQL warehouses
Customer-facing data products served from the same platform
evidence_quotes: 45% Cost savings after consolidating fragmented data systems
15x Faster time to market for new data products and features
35% Fewer production incidents due to improved data reliability

solution_components.name: Delta Lake
solution_components.role: Lakehouse storage holding the governed source-of-truth tables for content, ads, services, schedules, and user data

solution_components.name: Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines
solution_components.role: Ingestion and validation framework that lands data from all source systems into the governed Lakehouse

solution_components.name: Unity Catalog
solution_components.role: Cross-cloud governance for lineage, access control, sensitive-data tagging, and compliance across AWS and GCP

solution_components.name: Databricks SQL
solution_components.role: SQL warehouse access for Finance, Operations, and Product analytics on the unified Lakehouse

solution_components.name: Databricks Genie
solution_components.role: Natural-language querying for internal stakeholders against governed Lakehouse data

solution_components.name: Databricks Serverless Compute
solution_components.role: Auto-scaling compute that replaces always-on infrastructure across analytics workloads

data_flow: Source systems — content metadata, asset catalogs, advertising data, service streams, program schedules, and user data — land in Delta Lake via Lakeflow declarative pipelines. Unity Catalog governs lineage, access, and sensitive-data tagging across the multicloud AWS+GCP footprint. Internal users query the same governed Lakehouse through Databricks SQL warehouses, Genie natural-language sessions, and notebooks on serverless compute, while customer-facing data products (streaming analytics, ad attribution, usage and revenue reporting) are derived from the same source of truth.
integration_points: Streaming platforms (channel delivery and content performance analytics)
Ad-tech partners (ad intelligence and personalized attribution feeds)
Multicloud deployment across AWS and Google Cloud
Legacy systems being retired: Dataproc, Snowflake, and in-house platforms
